2605.10043.title: Personalizing LLMs with Binary Feedback: A Preference-Corrected Optimization Framework
2605.10043.paper_summary: Large Language Model (LLM) personalization aims to align model behaviors with individual user preferences. Existing methods often focus on isolated user histories, neglecting the essential role of inter-user differences. We propose C-BPO, a framework that personalizes LLMs via preference-calibrated binary signals. By treating target user data as positive feedback and other users' data as an auxiliary set of implicit negative signals, C-BPO captures distinct inter-user differences. To mitigate the preference overlap issue, where shared task knowledge is erroneously penalized, we derive an objective grounded in Positive-Unlabeled (PU) learning theory. This approach purifies negative signals by subtracting ``positive bias'', ensuring alignment with unique idiosyncrasies without compromising general helpfulness. Empirical experiments across various personalization tasks and backbone LLMs show C-BPO consistently outperforms baselines, demonstrating the efficacy of preference-calibrated binary signals in modeling inter-user differences.
2605.10043.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）個性化旨在使模型行為與個別用戶的偏好對齊。現有的方法通常專注於孤立的用戶歷史，忽略了用戶之間差異的重要角色。我們提出了C-BPO，一個通過偏好校準的二元信號來個性化LLM的框架。通過將目標用戶數據視為正向反饋，將其他用戶的數據視為隱含的負向信號輔助集，C-BPO捕捉到明顯的用戶間差異。為了減輕偏好重疊問題，即共享任務知識被錯誤懲罰，我們推導出一個基於正負樣本（PU）學習理論的目標。這種方法通過減去“正向偏差”來淨化負向信號，確保與獨特的特性對齊，同時不妨礙一般的有用性。在各種個性化任務和基礎LLM上的實證實驗顯示，C-BPO始終超越基準，證明了偏好校準的二元信號在建模用戶間差異中的有效性。
2605.10043.author: Xilai Ma et.al.
2605.10043.authors: Xilai Ma, Liye Zhao, Weijun Yao, Haibing Di, Wenya Wang, Jing Li
